data.narrative:
Pixxel is a Series B-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $95M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 5 public-market peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 3 same-stage private comparables in the sector: Aalyria ($1.3B), Hubble Network ($400M), and Sift ($274M).

Two valuation methods triangulate to a defensible range. Stage-progression methodology, applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implies $180.5M based on the $95M raised proxy. Peer-median analysis anchors to the $400M median valuation across the three same-sector, same-stage comparables, with a $274M–$1.3B spread reflecting material dispersion in the comp set. Public-market P/S anchoring is not applicable, as Pixxel's revenue is not on file; the sector median P/S of 53.2x across public peers cannot be deployed without revenue data.

Synthesized across the two applicable methods, the analysis supports a valuation range of $153M (low) to $460M (high), with a median point of $290M. The wide range reflects genuine uncertainty around Pixxel's revenue traction, unit economics, and near-term growth trajectory relative to the comp set. The high end of the range aligns with peer outliers like Aalyria; downside gravitates toward the stage-progression floor. Key sensitivities include actual revenue run-rate, customer concentration, and launch cadence execution.
